text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
CONFIRMATORY RESTAGING EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Okonkwo is a 63-year-old female with known stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), right kidney (pT3b pN1 M1), status post right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. She is currently receiving nivo monotherapy maintenance following induction with ipi/nivo (4 cycles; ipi permanently discontinued following Grade 2 immune-related colitis). Prior CT at approximately 3 months following systemic therapy initiation demonstrated mixed response with increase in target lesion 1 (right lower lobe dominant nodule, 2.1 cm from 1.8 cm baseline) and decrease in target lesions 2 and 3 (left and right lower lobe), with overall stable disease (sum of target lesion diameters -3% from baseline). Clinical question: pseudoprogression versus true progression of target lesion 1, and interval assessment of disease burden following continued Opdivo. Confirmatory scan performed 6 weeks after prior restaging examination.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed on a 64-slice scanner. Intravenous contrast was administered: 100 mL iohexol (Omnipaque 350) via antecubital vein injection using automated bolus tracking with hepatic portal venous phase acquisition for the abdomen and pelvis (70-second delay) and pulmonary arterial phase for the chest. Oral contrast: water only (negative oral contrast). Reconstructions: 1.25-mm axial, coronal, and sagittal multiplanar reformats. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, soft tissue windows, and bone windows reviewed. Dose modulation utilized. Comparison: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast from approximately 6 weeks prior (3-month restaging scan) and original pre-systemic therapy baseline CT.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Pleura:
Right lower lobe: The dominant target lesion (target lesion 1), previously measuring 1.8 cm at baseline and 2.1 cm at the 3-month scan, now measures 2.6 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 2.1 cm, representing a 24% increase from the prior scan and a 44% increase from the original pre-systemic therapy baseline of 1.8 cm). The lesion retains a spiculated, irregular morphology with ill-defined margins, consistent with malignant etiology. There is no associated cavitation or pleural tethering. This represents unambiguous progressive growth of target lesion 1, exceeding the 20% RECIST threshold from nadir.

Target lesion 2 (right lower lobe, separate nodule, medial segment): 0.6 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 0.7 cm at the 3-month scan, 0.9 cm at baseline). Stable to minimally decreased compared with prior.

Target lesion 3 (left lower lobe, superior segment): 0.5 cm (unchanged from the 3-month scan; decreased from 0.7 cm at baseline). Stable.

New finding: Two new pulmonary nodules are identified in the right middle lobe, measuring 0.6 cm and 0.4 cm in greatest axial dimension. These nodules were not identified on the prior 3-month scan or the original baseline examination and are new since the most recent prior imaging. The larger of the two is located in the medial segment of the right middle lobe; the smaller is in the lateral segment. Both are solid, non-calcified nodules with smooth borders. In the clinical context of known metastatic ccRCC, these are highly suspicious for new pulmonary metastases.

The previously noted two subcentimeter non-target nodules (bilateral, noted at baseline) remain stable and non-measurable; no significant interval change.

No cavitating nodules. No ground-glass opacities or consolidation to suggest infectious or inflammatory etiology. No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila:
No new mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. No significant interval change in mediastinal contour. Heart size normal. Pericardium unremarkable. No pericardial effusion. The main pulmonary artery is not enlarged. Thoracic aorta and major thoracic vessels are patent and unremarkable.

Chest Wall and Thoracic Skeleton:
No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions within the visualized thoracic skeleton (ribs, sternum, thoracic vertebral bodies, shoulder girdles). No pathological fractures.

Other:
Thyroid gland, to the extent imaged, appears unremarkable. No supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and contour. Hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal lesions. No hepatic metastases. No biliary ductal dilation. Portal vein is patent.

Gallbladder and Biliary System:
Gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No cholelithiasis. No biliary ductal dilation. Common bile duct measures within normal limits.

Spleen:
Spleen is normal in size and attenuation. No splenic lesions. No splenomegaly.

Pancreas:
Pancreas is normal in attenuation, morphology, and size. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid collections.

Adrenal Glands:
Status post right nephrectomy; the right adrenal gland was resected en bloc with the surgical specimen. Left adrenal gland is of normal morphology and attenuation. No left adrenal metastasis.

Kidney:
Status post right radical nephrectomy. The nephrectomy bed demonstrates expected post-surgical changes with no evidence of local recurrence. Surgical clips noted. No soft tissue mass at the right nephrectomy bed. The left kidney is solitary and compensatorily hypertrophied (longitudinal dimension 13.4 cm). Normal cortical thickness and enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No left renal mass. No perirenal stranding.

Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes:
New finding: A right paracaval lymph node is identified at the level of the right renal hilum, measuring 1.1 cm in short axis (1.3 cm in long axis). This node was not measurable on the prior 3-month scan and was not present on the original baseline CT. This represents a new retroperitoneal lymph node metastasis in a region previously cleared by retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. No left para-aortic, aortocaval, or common iliac lymphadenopathy. No inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Gastrointestinal Tract:
Small bowel loops are normal in caliber and wall thickness. Colon appears unremarkable. No bowel obstruction. No significant peritoneal thickening or ascites. Given the patient's prior history of immune-related colitis (now resolved), no residual bowel wall thickening or pericolonic stranding is noted.

Peritoneum and Mesentery:
No peritoneal implants. No ascites. No mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

Vascular Structures:
Abdominal aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilation. Inferior vena cava is patent with no filling defect; no intraluminal thrombus (status post resection of right renal vein tumor thrombus). Celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery are patent to the extent visualized.

PELVIS:

Bladder:
Urinary bladder is normally distended with a smooth wall. No intraluminal filling defects. No bladder mass.

Gynecologic Structures:
Uterus is normal in size and morphology for patient age. No adnexal masses. No pelvic free fluid.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Osseous Structures (Pelvis and Lumbar Spine):
No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions within the pelvis, sacrum, or visualized lumbar vertebral bodies. No pathological fracture. Moderate degenerative changes of the lumbar spine consistent with known spondylosis.

Soft Tissues:
No subcutaneous or intramuscular masses. The right flank surgical scar is noted without evidence of local recurrence or wound-related complication.

RECIST 1.1 RESPONSE ASSESSMENT:

Target Lesions:
- Target lesion 1 (RLL dominant nodule): 2.6 cm (baseline 1.8 cm; prior scan 2.1 cm)
- Target lesion 2 (RLL second nodule): 0.6 cm (baseline 0.9 cm; prior scan 0.7 cm)
- Target lesion 3 (LLL nodule): 0.5 cm (baseline 0.7 cm; prior scan 0.5 cm)
- Sum of target lesion diameters (current): 3.7 cm
- Sum of target lesion diameters (prior scan): 3.3 cm
- Sum of target lesion diameters (original baseline): 3.4 cm
- Change from baseline: +9% (increase)
- Change from nadir: +12% (increase from 3.3 cm nadir)

New Lesions:
- Two new right middle lobe pulmonary nodules (0.6 cm and 0.4 cm)
- New right paracaval retroperitoneal lymph node (1.1 cm short axis)

Overall RECIST 1.1 Assessment: PROGRESSIVE DISEASE — based on the identification of unequivocal new lesions (two new pulmonary nodules in the right middle lobe and a new retroperitoneal lymph node), which independently satisfy RECIST 1.1 criteria for progressive disease regardless of the change in sum of target lesion diameters.

IMPRESSION:

1. PROGRESSIVE DISEASE per RECIST 1.1: Confirmatory scan demonstrates unequivocal new pulmonary metastases (two right middle lobe nodules, 0.6 and 0.4 cm) and a new right paracaval retroperitoneal lymph node metastasis (1.1 cm short axis), establishing progressive disease by new lesion criteria.

2. Continued growth of target lesion 1 (right lower lobe dominant nodule): Now 2.6 cm (from 1.8 cm baseline, 44% increase; from 2.1 cm on prior scan, 24% increase). The temporal pattern of continued growth on two successive scans argues strongly against pseudoprogression.

3. Target lesions 2 and 3 remain stable to mildly decreased, demonstrating continued heterogeneous response to immunotherapy.

4. The right paracaval retroperitoneal lymph node (1.1 cm) represents new nodal disease in a region that was previously surgically cleared (RPLND performed at time of nephrectomy), consistent with regional lymphatic recurrence.

5. No hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or brain metastases identified on this examination.

6. Status post right radical nephrectomy: No local recurrence at the nephrectomy bed.

7. Solitary left kidney with compensatory hypertrophy; no hydronephrosis or new renal pathology.

RADIOLOGIST ATTESTATION:
Images reviewed and interpreted by the attending radiologist. Report finalized.
